chunk_summary: This section outlines an India-adapted 24-hour dietary recall script for assessing child feeding practices, emphasizing structured probing, portion estimation, and contextual factors to ensure comprehensive data collection.
learning_objectives: Understand the structure and purpose of the India-adapted 24-hour dietary recall interview script.
Learn to use targeted probes to capture detailed dietary intake, including meals, snacks, and supplements.
Develop skills to estimate portion sizes using household measures and natural units.
Recognize the importance of contextual factors like illness, travel, or fasting in dietary assessments.
key_facts: The script starts with a time anchor: 'Tell me everything your child ate or drank from when they woke up yesterday until they slept.'
Probing sequence includes six categories: meals/snacks, milk feeds, mixed dishes, forgotten items, supplements/medicines, and context.
Portion sizes are recorded using household measures (e.g., katori, glass, ladle) and natural units (e.g., number of idlis, chapatis).
For packaged foods, record brand, packet size, and consumed quantity.
If time is limited, prioritize energy-dense items, protein sources, fruits, vegetables, and liquid calories.
Close the interview with a recap and correction: 'What did I miss?'
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: A brief, standardized script improves completeness and reduces variability across interviewers.
document_key_topics: Contextual factors in child feeding evaluation
